title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft
label: mx1_transmute_20
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
The problem is a prefix-free coding problem where we must extend a set of existing binary strings to ensure that no string is a prefix of another, while minimizing the total length of the extended strings.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Given a finite set of binary strings S, construct a new set of binary strings S' such that for every string s in S, there exists a string s' in S' where s
final_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: Extend each string in S to the shortest possible prefix-free set.
JUSTIFICATION: Minimize lengths by extending strings to minimal unique suffixes.

run_level_variable_overrides.selected_prompt_text:
Transform the provided problem into a rigorous mathematical specification. Your goal is to strip away all narrative elements and provide a precise formal description of the input, the required properties of the solution, and the optimization objective. 

Your 'message_for_next_agent' must define:
1) The formal properties of the input elements (e.g., sets, sequences, or tree structures).
2) The logical invariants that must hold for a solution to be valid (e.g., prefix-free properties or uniqueness constraints).
3) The exact optimization criteria (e.g., lexicographical minimality or length minimality).
